events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to produce a JSON answer for a grounding electrode system inspection. The scenario: GEC spliced improperly. Work stage: rough-complete. Condition: installed-defective. We need to decide pass/fail/needs_more_info, risk, etc. Based on NEC 250.50-250.68, improper splicing of grounding electrode conductor is a violation. So decision: fail. Risk: high or critical? Improper grounding can be a safety hazard, so likely high. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective. Percent complete: maybe 90%? But it's rough-complete, so maybe 80-90%. Value: not applicable. Sound source: not applicable. Confidence: high. Event time, rate, order, workable: not needed. Findings: "GEC spliced improperly" is the defect. Actions: "Replace or repair splice per NEC 250.64(C) requirements." Rationale: NEC 250.64(C) requires continuous GEC without splice unless using irreversible compression-type connectors listed for grounding or exothermic welding. References: NEC 250.64(C). So I'll craft JSON accordingly.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Grounding electrode conductor (GEC) spliced improperly"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove improper splice and install a continuous GEC or use an irreversible compression connector listed for grounding, or exothermic welding, per NEC 250.64(C)"
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 250.64(C) requires the GEC to be continuous without splice unless spliced by irreversible compression-type connectors listed for grounding or by exothermic welding. An improper splice compromises the grounding path, creating a shock and fire hazard.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 250.64(C)"
  ]
}
